title: cyclosorus sambiranensis
intro: Cyclosorus sambiranensis, also known as the Madagascar fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae, characterized by its delicate fronds and distinctive leaf morphology. Traditionally, the Cyclosorus sambiranensis plant is used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat fever, digestive issues, and skin infections, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the Cyclosorus sambiranensis plant are often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
